text: Meet Stan. He has one thing due tomorrow, so tonight he scrubbed the whole kitchen, reorganized his desk, and answered every email. Anything but the actual task. And he felt awful the entire time. Here's the giveaway. Stan isn't lazy. Lazy is not even wanting to do the thing. Stan desperately wants to, and he still can't make himself start. Procrastination isn't laziness. It's almost the opposite. So, what is it really?
